text: {"matches":[{"type":"contradicts","fromIndex":0,"toIndex":1,"rationale":"Claim 0 asserts a proven preventive effect, while Claim 1 reports only a weak, non‑significant association, making the two statements mutually exclusive as stated."},{"type":"contradicts","fromIndex":0,"toIndex":3,"rationale":"Claim 0 says vitamin D prevention is proven, whereas Claim 3 says the finding still requires confirmation in randomized trials, directly opposing each other."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":1,"toIndex":2,"rationale":"Claim 1 describes an observed weak, non‑significant association; Claim 2 comments on the statistical uncertainty of that result, so both can be true but address different aspects."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":1,"toIndex":3,"rationale":"Claim 1 reports an observational association, while Claim 3 calls for confirmation in RCTs; they are compatible but concern different scopes of evidence."},{"type":"compatible-different-scope","fromIndex":2,"toIndex":3,"rationale":"Claim 2 suggests the result may be due to chance, and Claim 3 recommends further trials; both address uncertainty but focus on different explanations."}]}
